tagline: Step through. See the world live.
elevator_pitch: Portals is a pop-up museum experience featuring life-sized live video screens that connect visitors with real people in cities around the globe. Walk room to room, from Tokyo to Rome to Nairobi, sharing snacks, stories, and culture in real time with live translation built in.
problem: Most people will never visit the majority of countries on Earth. Virtual travel experiences feel flat and one-directional. There is no visceral, human way to connect with someone across the globe without booking a flight.
solution: Portals builds large-format live video installations in pop-up museum spaces. Each room is a real-time window into a different city, staffed by local cultural ambassadors. Visitors can talk, share snacks, and learn through live AI-powered translation, making cross-cultural connection feel immediate and tangible.
target_market: Urban millennials and Gen Z audiences (ages 18-40) who love experiential entertainment, pop-up events, and cultural discovery. Secondary markets include school field trips, corporate team-building, and tourism boards looking to promote their cities.
revenue_model: Ticket sales at $20-35 per visitor for timed entry sessions. Additional revenue from corporate event bookings, educational group packages, branded cultural partnerships (e.g. food brands sponsoring the snack exchange), and merchandise. City tourism boards pay placement fees to be featured as a portal destination.
key_features: Life-sized live video portals with sub-second sync to cities worldwide
AI-powered real-time translation enabling natural cross-language conversation
Curated cultural exchange kits with local snacks, artifacts, and conversation starters
Rotating monthly themes like street food, music, dance, and local crafts
competitive_advantage: Unlike static museum exhibits or VR headset experiences, Portals delivers genuine two-way human interaction at scale. The live element is irreplaceable by video or VR. The pop-up model keeps overhead low while generating social media buzz and scarcity-driven demand.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. experiential entertainment market is valued at over $12 billion. Pop-up museums like Museum of Ice Cream and Meow Wolf have each generated $50M+ in revenue. A network of Portals installations in 10 major cities could capture $30-50M annually at maturity.
go_to_market: Launch a single flagship Portals experience in a high-traffic city like New York or Los Angeles, partnering with two to three international cities. Generate buzz through influencer previews and earned media. Use timed-entry ticketing via platforms like Fever or Eventbrite to manage demand and create urgency.
risks: Time zone logistics make it difficult to have live connections with all cities during visitor hours
Reliance on stable high-bandwidth international video infrastructure
Pop-up venue leasing costs in major cities can be unpredictable and expensive
year_one_milestones: Launch flagship location with 6 portal rooms connecting to 6 international cities
Sell 150,000 tickets in the first 12 months at an average price of $25
Secure 3 corporate sponsors and 2 tourism board partnerships
startup_cost_range: $500K - $1.2M for first location (AV hardware, venue lease, international setup, staffing, marketing)
